# Advanced client-server interaction tests

# Set aggressive timeouts for this test
env MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT=3s

# Test complex JSON processing with mcpspy
exec bash -c 'printf "%s\n%s\n%s\n" "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 1, \"method\": \"tools/list\"}" "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 2, \"method\": \"tools/call\", \"params\": {\"name\": \"test\"}}" "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 3, \"method\": \"resources/list\"}" | mcpspy --pretty'
stdout '"tools/list"'
stdout '"tools/call"'
stdout '"resources/list"'

# Test mcpdiff with different MCP trace files
! exec mcpdiff trace1.mcp trace2.mcp

# Test mcpspy help functionality
exec mcpspy --help
stderr 'Usage'

-- trace1.mcp --
mcp-send {"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "method": "initialize"} # 1000.123

-- trace2.mcp --
mcp-send {"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1, "method": "tools/list"} # 1000.123
